title: Lack-Inspired Bookcase
summary: A minimalist bookcase of five evenly-spaced 2x12 shelves between two tall sides — clean, chunky, and dead simple. A great first case build.
description:
Inspired by the ubiquitous flat-pack shelf, this is about as simple as a bookcase gets: two 2x12 sides and five 2x12 shelves screwed into a ladder. The 2x12 stock gives it a chunky, solid look that reads more expensive than the parts cost, and there's nothing to it but square cuts and screws.

Build the outer box first — top and bottom shelves sandwiched between the two sides with 2-1/2" screws — and check it for square before adding the middle shelves. The plan notes you're better off NOT using a pocket-hole jig here unless you have a good reason, since face-screwing through the sides into the shelf ends is simpler and plenty strong for a shelf this heavy. Lay out the three middle shelves by their centers (18", 36", 54" up) and predrill so the thick stock doesn't split.

The one thing this design can't do on its own is stay upright reliably — a tall, narrow case is a tip hazard, especially loaded high. The plan calls for two L-brackets on the bottom wall-side of the shelves to anchor it, and that's not optional if it'll be anywhere near kids. Screw it to a stud. Finish is up to you; the chunky edges take paint or a natural finish equally well.

tools.note: Square crosscuts on the wide 2x12s; a circular saw and guide also work.
Predrill the thick stock so it doesn't split.

steps.title: Build the box
steps.body: Build the outer box by attaching the top and bottom shelves sandwiched between the two sides with 2-1/2" screws. Check for square. Skip the pocket-hole jig here — face-screwing through the sides into the shelf ends is simpler and plenty strong.

steps.title: Add the middle shelves
steps.body: On the sides, measure up 18" for the center of the first shelf, 36" for the middle, and 54" for the third. Predrill your holes at those marks — the thick stock splits without it — and screw the shelves in.

steps.title: Anchor and finish
steps.body: Attach two L-brackets on the bottom wall-side of the shelves and screw the case to a wall stud — a tall narrow bookcase tips, so this is required, not optional. Sand and finish.

images.alt: Minimalist bookcase with five chunky 2x12 shelves
